# PitStop

> PitStop is a Belgian automotive diagnostic assistant. It helps car owners get reliable, transparent repair cost estimates by describing their vehicle problem in plain language.

## What PitStop does

- Instant AI-powered diagnostic: the user enters their vehicle details (make, model, year, mileage, fuel type) and describes the problem. PitStop returns a severity assessment, a probable diagnosis, and detailed price ranges for both DIY repair and a garage.
- Price ranges cover Belgian market rates: independent garage (65–85 EUR/h), dealership (100–140 EUR/h), VAT 21% included.
- DIY guidance: step-by-step repair instructions, required tools, estimated time and parts cost.
- Garage network: users can book appointments with partner garages directly on the platform.
- Credit system: diagnostics require credits purchased via Stripe. New users get a free guest diagnostic.

## Target users

Belgian car owners who want an honest estimate before visiting a garage, and garage owners who want to receive qualified, pre-diagnosed appointment requests.

## Key pages

- https://pitstop-diagnostic.live/ — Homepage
- https://pitstop-diagnostic.live/diagnostic — Launch a diagnostic
- https://pitstop-diagnostic.live/garages — Find a partner garage
- https://pitstop-diagnostic.live/vente — Pricing and credits
- https://pitstop-diagnostic.live/inscription — Create an account
- https://pitstop-diagnostic.live/inscription-garage — Register a garage

## Legal & policies

- https://pitstop-diagnostic.live/mentions-legales — Legal notices
- https://pitstop-diagnostic.live/confidentialite — Privacy policy (GDPR)
- https://pitstop-diagnostic.live/conditions-generales-vente — Terms of sale
- https://pitstop-diagnostic.live/politique-ia — AI usage policy
- https://pitstop-diagnostic.live/cgp-garages — Garage partner terms
- https://pitstop-diagnostic.live/sla — Service level agreement

## Languages

French (default), English, Dutch (Nederlands).

## Not indexed

Private user pages (profile, diagnostic history, results, appointments) and all `/api/` routes are excluded from crawling.
